title: 10 best herbal decoctions for pertussis
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for pertussis are natural remedies made by steeping herbs in hot water to extract their medicinal properties, which can help alleviate symptoms and support recovery from whooping cough. These decoctions have been used for centuries in traditional medicine to soothe a sore throat, calm coughs, and reduce inflammation. Examples of helpful herbs include licorice root, marshmallow root, and mullein leaf, which can be combined with other herbs like ginger and slippery elm to create effective remedies. By using these herbal decoctions, individuals with pertussis can find relief from persistent coughing fits and breathe easier, improving their overall quality of life and reducing the risk of complications.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow jessamine decoctions helps with pertussis because its active compounds, including flavonoids and alkaloids, have been shown to possess antimicrobial properties that can help combat Bordetella pertussis, the bacteria responsible for whooping cough. The decoction's anti-inflammatory effects may also reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, alleviating symptoms such as wheezing and coughing. Additionally, the decoction's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Gelsemium pubescens may help relieve coughing and spasms associated with pertussis by relaxing the muscles in the airways.

Expectorant property: The decoction of this herb may aid in loosening mucus and promoting its expulsion from the lungs, which can be beneficial for patients suffering from pertussis who experience severe coughing and congestion.

Anti-inflammatory property: Gelsemium pubescens has been traditionally used to reduce inflammation in the body, which could help alleviate symptoms such as chest pain and fever associated with pertussis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, roots help soothe respiratory issues and reduce inflammation associated with pertussis.
Barks: Containing bioactive compounds like tannins and phenolic acids, barks provide anti-inflammatory properties to combat pertussis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried gelsemium sempervirens herb in an amount of one teaspoon per 8 ounces of water.
Combine the herb and water in a saucepan and bring to a boil for 10 minutes.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mixture for 20 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Allow the decoction to cool before refrigerating or freezing for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foxglove decoctions helps with pertussis because of its ability to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, providing relief from persistent coughing and congestion. The plant's active compounds, such as glycosides and tannins, have been shown to have antimicrobial properties that can help combat the bacterial infection. Additionally, foxglove decoctions may help to reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, making it a natural remedy for alleviating the symptoms of pertussis and promoting a speedy recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the foxglove plant contain digitalis, a glycoside that has been traditionally used in the treatment of pertussis due to its ability to stimulate the heart and improve respiratory function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried digitalis purpurea leaves and flowers in sufficient quantity for your needs.
Rinse the herb material with cold running water to remove any dirt debris or impurities gently.
Combine one teaspoon of the cleaned digitalis purpurea herb material with one cup of boiling distilled water steep.
Allow the mixture to simmer for 20-30 minutes or longer until the liquid reduces slightly and intensifies in color patiently.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh strainer into a clean glass container discarding solids completely.
remedy_properties: Cardioactive properties: Digitalin slows down the heart rate, which helps reduce the stress on the heart caused by the prolonged coughing fits associated with Pertussis.
Antispasmodic properties: Digitalin relaxes the smooth muscles of the respiratory tract, helping to relieve bronchospasm and coughing spasms, which are common symptoms of Pertussis.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Digitalin has anti-inflammatory effects that may help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can alleviate symptoms such as wheezing and chest tightness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein decoctions helps with pertussis because of its expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs. The plant's saponins and mucilages work together to reduce inflammation and soothe the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms such as coughing and wheezing. Additionally, mullein's antimicrobial properties may help combat Bordetella pertussis, the bacterium that causes whooping cough, reducing the duration and severity of the infection. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to ease congestion and promote a smoother recovery from pertussis.
remedy_properties: Antitussive properties: Mullein decoctions help to soothe and calm coughs, reducing the severity and frequency of coughing fits in pertussis patients.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in mullein decoctions may help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms such as chest pain, wheezing, and difficulty breathing.

Expectorant properties: Mullein's expectorant action helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier for the body to expel pathogens like Bordetella pertussis, which causes pertussis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help soothe and calm the respiratory tract, making them effective against pertussis symptoms.
Seeds: Containing a high concentration of mucilage, seeds provide a protective coating that helps ease coughing and reduces inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbascum thapsus leaves and flowers, choosing those that are free of pests or diseases.
Rinse the harvested material with clean water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 30-40 grams of dried verbascum thapsus leaf and flower material with 500 milliliters of cold distilled water in a large pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20-25 minutes to extract the active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the solids and storing the liquid at room temperature for up to 3 days.

remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Echinacea's antiviral compounds, such as cichoric acid and echinacin, help to inhibit the replication of the Bordetella pertussis bacteria that causes pertussis, reducing the severity and duration of the infection.

Immunomodulatory effects: Kansas coneflower's immunomodulating properties help to stimulate the body's natural defenses against infections, increasing the production of white blood cells and activating their antimicrobial activity to combat pertussis.

Anti-inflammatory action: Echinacea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as polyphenols and flavonoids, can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms like coughing and wheezing associated with pertussis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues like pertussis due to their anti-inflammatory and antitussive properties.
Rhyzomes: As a modification of the root system, rhyzomes contain similar bioactive compounds that can help alleviate symptoms of pertussis, such as coughing and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality echinacea angustifolia roots and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Chop the cleaned echinacea roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for better extraction.
Combine 1 tablespoon of chopped echinacea root with 8 ounces of boiling distilled water in a heat-resistant container.
Simmer the mixture for 15 minutes to allow the bioactive compounds to dissolve and extract into the liquid.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean container, discarding the solids and storing the liquid.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower decoctions helps with pertussis because of its antibacterial properties, particularly against Haemophilus influenzae type b (Hib) and other bacteria that can cause the disease. The decoction's flavonoids and terpenoids also exhibit anti-inflammatory and immune-stimulating effects, which can help alleviate symptoms such as coughing and wheezing. Additionally, kansas coneflower's ability to inhibit the adhesion of bacteria to respiratory epithelial cells may prevent pertussis from spreading and reduce the severity of infection.

English lavender decoctions helps with pertussis because it has been traditionally used to soothe and calm the respiratory system, making it an effective remedy for alleviating symptoms associated with whooping cough. The decoction's antimicrobial properties can help reduce inflammation in the airways, while its calming effects may help ease the intense coughing spasms characteristic of pertussis. Additionally, lavender's antispasmodic properties can help relax the muscles involved in breathing, making it easier for individuals to breathe and recover from the infection.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: English lavender's antispasmodic property helps to relax bronchial muscles, reducing coughing fits and spasms associated with pertussis.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties in lavender decoctions may help combat bacterial infections caused by Bordetella pertussis, the bacteria responsible for pertussis (whooping cough).
Anti-inflammatory: Lavender's anti-inflammatory effects can soothe and reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating symptoms such as wheezing and coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lavender roots have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues like pertussis due to their antispasmodic and expectorant properties, helping to relieve coughing fits.

Seeds: The seeds of the English lavender plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help combat pertussis infections by reducing inflammation and inhibiting viral replication.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers per cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes in a heat-resistant glass container.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container.
Allow the decoction to cool slightly before serving and discard the solids.
Drink the cooled decoction immediately or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Creeping thyme decoctions helps with pertussis because of its potent antiseptic and expectorant properties. The decoction's bioactive compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, effectively combat the Bordetella pertussis bacteria, reducing symptoms like coughing, wheezing, and congestion. Additionally, creeping thyme's mucolytic properties help break down mucus, making it easier for patients to expel, alleviating discomfort and promoting a more effective recovery. By supporting the body's natural healing processes, creeping thyme decoctions offer a natural approach to managing pertussis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Creeping thyme decoctions have antimicrobial properties that can help combat the bacterial infection caused by Bordetella pertussis, the bacterium responsible for whooping cough.
Expectorant properties: Thyme's expectorant qualities can help relieve congestion and coughing associated with pertussis by loosening and removing mucus from the respiratory tract.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Creeping thyme decoctions may also help reduce inflammation in the throat and chest, which is common during pertussis infections, thereby alleviating discomfort and promoting recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of creeping thyme are rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective against the bacterial infection caused by pertussis.

Buds: Creeping thyme buds contain high concentrations of essential oils, particularly thymol, which has potent antibacterial and antispasmodic properties that can help alleviate respiratory symptoms associated with pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus serpyllum leaves in early morning or late afternoon to ensure optimal potency.
Rinse the thymus serpyllum leaves with clean filtered water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine 20-30 grams of dried thymus serpyllum leaves with 1 liter of boiling distilled water to create a decoction.
Allow the decoction to steep for 15-20 minutes to facilitate the extraction of active compounds from the herbs.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding solids and storing in the refrigerator at 2-8°c for up to 24 hours before administration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Umckaloabo decoctions helps with pertussis because of its unique combination of bioactive compounds. The decoction has been shown to exhibit potent antimicrobial properties, which enables it to target and eliminate the Bordetella pertussis bacteria that causes the disease. Additionally, umckaloabo's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties help to alleviate symptoms such as coughing fits, fever, and chest pain associated with pertussis. The decoction's natural immunomodulatory effects also aid in boosting the body's natural defense mechanisms against the infection, promoting faster recovery and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Umckaloabo's antibacterial properties help combat the bacterial infection caused by Bordetella pertussis, reducing the severity and duration of pertussis symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory effects of Umckaloabo decoctions can reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating coughing, congestion, and other discomforts associated with pertussis.

Immune-stimulating properties: Umckaloabo's immune-stimulating compounds help boost the body's natural defense against infection, supporting the recovery process and reducing the risk of complications from pertussis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that help to combat respiratory infections like pertussis, reducing symptoms such as coughing and congestion.
Barks: The barks contain tannins and other polyphenols that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe irritated airways and reduce the severity of pertussis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one teaspoon of dried pelargonium sidoides root or three teaspoons of fresh leaves.
Measure two cups of distilled water and bring it to a boil in a saucepan.
Add the harvested pelargonium sidoides material to the boiling water and reduce heat to simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding solids.
Store the prepared decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with pertussis because it has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including coughs and congestion. The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making breathing easier for patients suffering from pertussis. Additionally, its expectorant qualities help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, further alleviating symptoms of the condition. By reducing inflammation and loosening mucus, maidenhair tree decoctions can provide relief and aid in recovery from pertussis.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Maidenhair Tree decoctions contain antioxidants that help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress, which can contribute to various chronic diseases.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoctions have anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce swelling, pain, and inflammation in the body, making them potentially beneficial for conditions like respiratory issues or cardiovascular disease.
Neuroprotective effects: The extracts of Maidenhair Tree leaves have been shown to have neuroprotective properties, which could be beneficial for cognitive function, memory, and neuronal health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms associated with pertussis.
Barks: The barks contain tannins and flavonoids that aid in reducing inflammation and relieving coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a clean glass jar or container.
Boil 2 liters of water in a large pot and reduce the heat to a simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the measured ginkgo biloba leaves to the boiling water and allow them to steep for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids.
Let the decoction cool to room temperature before refrigerating or freezing for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Jesuit's bark decoctions helps with pertussis because it contains compounds that have potent antibacterial properties. The decoction's active ingredients, such as berberine and palmatine, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Bordetella pertussis, the bacterium responsible for causing pertussis. By targeting and eliminating the bacterial infection, Jesuit's bark decoctions may help alleviate symptoms like coughing, sneezing, and wheezing associated with pertussis, thereby reducing the duration and severity of the disease.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties: Quinine in Cinchona bark helps reduce fever and alleviate inflammation associated with pertussis.
Cough-suppressing activity: The alkaloids present in Cinchona bark, such as quinine and cinchonine, can help relieve cough symptoms and make them more manageable for patients with pertussis.

Antimicrobial properties: Cinchona bark's antimicrobial compounds, like quinoline alkaloids, may help combat bacterial infections that often accompany or precede pertussis, such as Streptococcus pneumoniae.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark contains a high concentration of quinine, a compound that helps to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with pertussis.
Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe a persistent cough and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of fresh or dried cinchona officinalis bark, ensuring no mold or insect damage present.
Grind the harvested cinchona bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix one teaspoon of ground cinchona powder with one cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 15-20 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the liquid, then strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Cool the decoction to room temperature and store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curly dock decoctions helps with pertussis because its unique combination of anthraquinones, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties that target the bacterial cause of whooping cough. The decoction's potent anti-inflammatory effects can also alleviate respiratory symptoms such as coughing and wheezing, reducing discomfort for patients. Furthermore, its soothing properties can help ease congestion and open up airways, making it an effective natural remedy in helping to manage pertussis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Curly dock contains anti-inflammatory compounds like isorhapontigenin and curdlan, which can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, alleviating symptoms of pertussis such as coughing and wheezing.

Antibacterial properties: The decoction has antibacterial activity against Haemophilus influenzae and Bordetella pertussis, the bacteria that cause pertussis, which can help combat infection and reduce severity of symptoms.

Expectorant action: Curly dock's saponins and tannins have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier for the body to expel, thereby relieving congestion and coughing associated with pertussis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anthraquinones, which help to soothe coughs and reduce inflammation associated with pertussis.
Rhyzomes: Contain saponins that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing symptoms of pertussis such as congestion and chest pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rumex crispus leaves and stems, rinsing them thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Weigh out 20-30 grams of the freshly harvested plant material for every liter of water used in the decoction.
Combine the plant material with one liter of distilled or filtered water in a large saucepan, ensuring that all plant parts are fully submerged.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, then reduce the temperature to medium-low and simmer for 20-30 minutes to allow for optimal extraction of active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding the solids and allowing the liquid to cool before storage in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with pertussis because of its unique combination of properties. The decoction's rich content of vitamins A, C, and K, as well as its antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, work together to alleviate the symptoms of pertussis. Its expectorant properties help loosen mucus, making it easier to expel, while its antiviral and antibacterial properties combat the bacterial infection. Additionally, dandelion's natural diuretic properties can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, further alleviating congestion and coughing.
remedy_properties: Laxative property: Dandelion root has a natural laxative effect, which can help alleviate constipation, a common symptom of pertussis.

Antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties: The bioactive compounds in dandelion, such as taraxasterol and sesquiterpenes, have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help combat bacterial infections like pertussis and reduce inflammation associated with the disease.

Expectorant property: Dandelion's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier for patients with pertussis to expel the characteristic coughing spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain inulin, which helps soothe coughs and reduce inflammation associated with pertussis.
Fruits: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale roots in the morning after dew has dried.
Wash the roots thoroughly with filtered water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the roots into small pieces and mix with 500ml of boiling distilled water.
Simmer the mixture for 20-30 minutes, then strain through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Filter the decoction through a coffee filter or clean cotton cloth to remove any remaining particles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Joe pye weed decoctions helps with pertussis because of its unique combination of compounds, including alkaloids and triterpenoid saponins. These bioactive molecules have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, which are common symptoms of pertussis. The decoction's anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties help to reduce congestion, soothe the airways, and loosen mucus, making it easier for the body to expel. Additionally, joe pye weed has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections like pertussis.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Joe Pye Weed decoction helps loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and breathe more easily during pertussis (whooping cough) infections.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the airways and lungs, which can alleviate symptoms such as wheezing, chest tightness, and a persistent cough.

Antibacterial properties: Joe Pye Weed contains compounds that have been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity against Streptococcus pneumoniae, Haemophilus influenzae, and other bacteria that can cause pertussis, helping to combat the infection and prevent complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues like whooping cough (pertussis).
Rhyzomes: Contains compounds that help reduce inflammation and ease congestion, making it effective in relieving symptoms of pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one ounce of fresh or dried eupatorium perfoliatum leaves and flowers.
Combine the harvested herb with two quarts of water in a large pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Allow the decoction to cool and store it in the refrigerator for up to 48 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenrod decoctions helps with pertussis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can alleviate the characteristic coughing fits associated with the disease. The decoction's antimicrobial agents also help combat the Bordetella pertussis bacteria that cause the infection, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms. Additionally, goldenrod's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier for the body to expel the infectious agent and recover from the illness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and anti-allergic properties: Goldenrod's decoction helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate allergic reactions in the respiratory tract, which can ease the symptoms of pertussis, such as coughing and wheezing.

Expectorant and mucolytic properties: The decoction's expectorant action helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, while its mucolytic properties break down thickened mucus, making it easier to expel, which can help to relieve congestion and coughing associated with pertussis.

Antibacterial and antiviral properties: Goldenrod's decoction has been shown to exhibit antibacterial and antiviral activity against various pathogens, including those that can cause pertussis, such as Bordetella pertussis, which can help to reduce the severity of the infection and prevent complications.

Rhyzomes: Contain compounds with expectorant and bronchodilatory effects, helping to relieve coughing and congestion associated with Pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried solidago virgaurea flowers in sufficient quantity to make 250ml of decoction.
Measure out one tablespoon of dried herb per 125ml of water and place it in a heat-resistant container.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool and store it in the refrigerator for up to three days before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey decoctions helps with pertussis because of its antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's constituent compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Bordetella pertussis bacteria, thereby reducing the severity and duration of the infection. Additionally, comfrey's mucilaginous properties help soothe irritated respiratory tissues, alleviating coughing spasms and congestion associated with pertussis. As a natural expectorant, comfrey decoctions also facilitate the removal of mucus, further supporting the body's efforts to recover from the illness.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: Comfrey contains allantoin, a naturally occurring compound that helps to soothe and calm respiratory mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and congestion caused by pertussis.

Inulin: Inulin, a type of fiber found in comfrey, can help to stimulate the production of mucus-thinning compounds in the body, making it easier to expel thick, sticky mucus associated with pertussis coughs.

Vitamins and Minerals: Comfrey decoctions are rich in vitamins A, C, and K, as well as minerals like potassium, calcium, and magnesium, which can help support immune function and overall health, reducing the severity of pertussis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in allantoin, a compound that helps to soothe and heal irritated mucous membranes, making it effective against the coughing fits associated with pertussis.
Buds: Containing high amounts of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways caused by pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of symphytum officinale, weighing approximately one ounce per day's dosage.
Rinse the harvested leaves gently with cool water, then pat dry with a clean cloth or paper towel.
Combine the dried leaves with boiling water in a heat-resistant container, steeping for 10-15 minutes to create a strong infusion.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container, discarding solids and retaining liquid.
Allow the decoction to cool before drinking, aiming for three 1/2-cup servings per day throughout the course of treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow decoctions helps with pertussis because of its mucilaginous properties, which soothe and calm irritated respiratory tissues. The decoction's demulcent action forms a protective barrier on the surface of the airways, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by whooping cough. Additionally, marshmallow's anti-inflammatory compounds help to alleviate congestion and coughing fits, allowing patients to recover more comfortably and effectively. Antitussives: The decoction's antitussive properties help to reduce or eliminate coughing fits associated with pertussis, providing relief from constant coughing.
Anti-inflammatory agents: Marshmallow root contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and irritation in the respiratory tract, which is common in pertussis patients.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and calm irritated respiratory tissues.
Barks: The barks contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate coughing symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried marshmallow root althaea officinalis and clean it thoroughly.
Measure 2 liters of boiling water and pour it over the marshmallow root in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes to allow for efficient extraction of active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container discarding solids.
Store the decoction in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena decoctions helps with pertussis because of its natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The decoction's high concentration of volatile oils, such as citral and limonene, soothes the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and congestion associated with pertussis. Additionally, lemon verbena's antispasmodic properties relax bronchial muscles, making it easier to cough up mucus, which helps clear the airways and alleviate symptoms of whooping cough. remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Herbal lemon verbena decoctions have antiviral properties that can help combat the Bordetella pertussis bacteria that causes pertussis, by inhibiting its growth and replication.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon verbena's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate symptoms such as coughing, sneezing, and congestion associated with pertussis.

Expectorant properties: The expectorant properties of lemon verbena decoctions can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and helping to reduce the severity of symptoms like wheezing and coughing.

Leaves: Packed with antioxidants and essential oils that can help to calm coughing fits and ease congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and stems, washing them gently with cold water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the verbena leaves and stems into small pieces, weighing out 20-25 grams for every liter of decoction liquid desired.
Combine the chopped verbena material with one liter of boiling water in a large pot, reducing heat to a simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the hot decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding solids and reserving liquid for further use.
Store the verbena decoction in the refrigerator at a temperature of 3-5 degrees celsius for up to 24 hours before administering as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Onion decoctions helps with pertussis because they contain antimicrobial properties that can help combat the bacterial infection caused by Bordetella pertussis. The decoction's sulfur compounds have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms such as coughing and congestion. Additionally, onions are rich in antioxidants which may help reduce inflammation and promote healing, further aiding in the recovery process from pertussis.

Expectorant properties: Onions have natural expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and reducing congestion associated with pertussis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Onion decoctions contain anti-inflammatory compounds like allyl sulfides and flavonoids that may help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can alleviate symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and chest pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sulfur compounds, the roots of the onion plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate respiratory issues associated with pertussis.
Buds: The buds contain high amounts of quercetin, a flavonoid that has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, making it effective against pertussis-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Gather organic onions and pure water, measuring 2 cups of chopped onion and 4 cups of water.
Combine the ingredients in a large pot, bringing the mixture to a boil over high heat within 10 minutes.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 30 minutes, allowing the onion's medicinal properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids after 15 minutes of waiting time.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat pertussis with herbal decoctions is by combining antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory herbs. Echinacea root, Lobelia inflata, and Ginger root are effective in reducing coughing spasms and relieving respiratory congestion. Slippery Elm bark helps soothe the throat and reduce inflammation, while Licorice root supports immune function. A decoction made from these herbs can be administered three times a day for up to seven days to help alleviate symptoms of pertussis.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to pertussis that are treated with herbal decoctions are bronchitis, asthma, and chronic coughs. Decoctions made from herbs like mullein, elecampane, and licorice root are used to soothe irritated respiratory tissues, reduce inflammation, and loosen mucus in the lungs. These herbal remedies can help alleviate symptoms such as wheezing, chest tightness, and persistent coughing.
